classification.loc_class: QC - Physics: plasma and electromagnetic fields

theory.canonical_statement: ID3373 records Capitaldelta Capacitance Relation as a source-extracted canonical equation.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `00_Engine\Lemma-Maker\Prism\refactored_derivations.tex` for registry alignment and later derivation.

latex_blocks: \[Omega] - \[Omega]0]^2/(2 \[CapitalDelta]\[Omega]^2)] Y[l, m][g]; psim = (a^\dagger)^nflux/Sqrt[nflux!] |0\rangle; psip = (1/Sqrt[nL! nR!]) ((\[AHat]_L^\dagger)^nL (\[AHat]_R^\dagger)^nR |0,0\rangle + Exp[I \[CurlyPhi]helix] (\[AHat]_R^\dagger)^nL (\[AHat]_L^\dagger)^nR |0,0\rangle); TensorProduct[psig, psim, psip] ]; \end{implementation} \section{Green’s Functions and Boundary Effects} This section collects analytic tools for computing vector potentials and field configurations. We define a modified Green’s function on a half–space with spherical boundary, decompose the vector potential into classical and quantum contributions, and identify the helicity–preserving transformation. \subsecti ...
canonical_equation.source_section_latex: \[Omega] - \[Omega]0]^2/(2 \[CapitalDelta]\[Omega]^2)] Y[l, m][g]; psim = (a^\dagger)^nflux/Sqrt[nflux!] |0\rangle; psip = (1/Sqrt[nL! nR!]) ((\[AHat]_L^\dagger)^nL (\[AHat]_R^\dagger)^nR |0,0\rangle + Exp[I \[CurlyPhi]helix] (\[AHat]_R^\dagger)^nL (\[AHat]_L^\dagger)^nR |0,0\rangle); TensorProduct[psig, psim, psip] ]; \end{implementation} \section{Green’s Functions and Boundary Effects} This section collects analytic tools for computing vector potentials and field configurations. We define a modified Green’s function on a half–space with spherical boundary, decompose the vector potential into classical and quantum contributions, and identify the helicity–preserving transformation. \subsecti ...

blocks.latex: \[Omega] - \[Omega]0]^2/(2 \[CapitalDelta]\[Omega]^2)] Y[l, m][g]; psim = (a^\dagger)^nflux/Sqrt[nflux!] |0\rangle; psip = (1/Sqrt[nL! nR!]) ((\[AHat]_L^\dagger)^nL (\[AHat]_R^\dagger)^nR |0,0\rangle + Exp[I \[CurlyPhi]helix] (\[AHat]_R^\dagger)^nL (\[AHat]_L^\dagger)^nR |0,0\rangle); TensorProduct[psig, psim, psip] ]; \end{implementation} \section{Green’s Functions and Boundary Effects} This section collects analytic tools for computing vector potentials and field configurations.
